events.title: Israel drone attack wounds hospital workers in Gaza
events.desc: An Israeli drone strike on Al-Kamal Adwan Hospital in Gaza wounded several workers. The Palestinian Ministry of Health condemned the attack, calling it part of Israel's systematic targeting of healthcare facilities. This incident underscores the ongoing violence and its impact on critical infrastructure in the region.

events.title: Russian attacks kill four in Ukraine
events.desc: Russian shelling in Kramatorsk resulted in four fatalities, including a teenager, and injured nine others. This attack highlights the continued kinetic exchanges in the Ukraine conflict, with civilian casualties being a persistent concern.

events.title: US and Iran trade strikes over Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The US military conducted strikes on missile sites and command centers in Iran, following earlier attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz which Washington attributed to Tehran. Iran claimed to have attacked US bases in response. This exchange marks a significant escalation in tensions between the two nations over maritime security.

events.title: Trump threatens more strikes on Iran
events.desc: US President Donald Trump stated that the US would "probably" strike Iran again following recent attacks in the Strait of Hormuz. He warned that any future action would be swift. This rhetoric signals a continued willingness to employ kinetic force in the ongoing conflict.

events.title: Headteacher among four killed in Israeli attack on Lebanon
events.desc: An Israeli attack in Lebanon killed four people, including a headteacher and three women travelling in a car. This incident represents the deadliest attack on Lebanon since a recent ceasefire, raising concerns about a potential resurgence of hostilities.

events.title: US and Iran agree to continue talks, ceasefire over
events.desc: President Donald Trump announced that the US has agreed to further negotiations with Iran after Tehran requested them. However, Trump reiterated that the ceasefire previously in place between the two nations is now over. This indicates a complex diplomatic situation with ongoing kinetic actions.

events.title: Qatari delegation in Iran to boost mediation role
events.desc: A Qatari delegation visited Iran, reportedly aiming to bolster Doha's role as a mediator following recent escalations in the Gulf. The visit follows Qatari accusations against Iran regarding an incident in the Strait of Hormuz and subsequent US actions.

events.title: UN agency rejects Iran's claim of sovereignty over Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The UN shipping agency's governing council agreed that countries should reject Iran's attempts to assert sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz and its decision to establish a body to control traffic. This statement comes amid recent US-Iran hostilities in the waterway.

events.title: Bomb explosions in Damascus during French President's visit
events.desc: Eighteen people were injured in Damascus by bomb explosions that occurred during a visit by French President Macron. State television reported that Syrian President Assad welcomed Macron at the presidential palace amidst the blasts, indicating a significant security incident.

events.title: Israeli police officer filmed using excessive force in West Bank
events.desc: An Israeli police officer was filmed throwing a stun grenade into a car in the West Bank. The Israeli police force acknowledged the officer acted "not in accordance with procedure" and has launched an investigation, highlighting potential misconduct and tensions in the region.

events.title: Palestinians mourn Gaza World Cup screenings organiser killed in Israeli strike
events.desc: Mohammed al-Wahidi, an aid worker who organized World Cup screenings in Gaza and became a prominent humanitarian figure during the Israel-Hamas war, was killed in an Israeli strike. His death highlights the ongoing impact of the conflict on civilians and humanitarian efforts.

events.title: Iran nuclear and military damage revealed after satellite images released
events.desc: Restricted satellite images have revealed damage to Iranian nuclear and military sites, including ballistic missile infrastructure, nuclear facilities, and naval bases. This suggests successful strikes targeting key Iranian assets.

events.title: US eases export controls to UAE
events.desc: The U.S. Department of Commerce has relaxed export controls for the United Arab Emirates, making it easier to export military items, commercial satellites, and spacecraft. Additionally, the UAE and approved companies will have license-free access to advanced computing items. This policy change could have implications for regional security and technology transfer.

events.title: Israel warns US of new Iran plot to assassinate Trump
events.desc: Israel has reportedly warned the United States of a new Iranian plot to assassinate former President Donald Trump. US officials are currently assessing the intelligence provided by Israel, indicating a potential high-level threat.
